targeted_tests: 29 pass (apps/api pytest: test_analytics.py + test_applications_tracker.py + test_workspaces.py, 29 passed / 0 failed, evidence timestamp 2026-07-18T17:1x — re-run twice, deterministic). Also ran frontend vitest for the 4 changed/new test files: 29 pass / 0 fail.

per_check.name: Canonical helper correctness
per_check.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] apps/api/app/routers/analytics.py:31-68 get_application_counts() returns total=COUNT(*) (all statuses) and submitted=COUNT(*) FILTER (status<>'draft'), matching the orchestrator ruling. All 3 named call sites now use it exclusively: funnel() line 87 (`applied = get_application_counts(...)['submitted']`, job_filter applied), market_pulse() lines 333-341 (`f_last_month = get_application_counts(..., ' AND "createdAt" >= NOW() - INTERVAL \'30 days\'')['submitted']`), _dashboard() line 619 (`total_apps = get_application_counts(cur, user_id, app_filter)['total']`). No divergent inline Application-count queries remain in analytics.py for these three functions (grep confirmed only get_application_counts issues a raw total/submitted COUNT against "Application"). period_clause is only ever built via _period_clause() (whitelists period in {7d,30d,90d,all}, interpolates a dict-controlled integer `days`, never the raw `period` string) or a hardcoded literal 30-day window in market_pulse — no SQL-injection surface, confirmed by direct code read.

per_check.name: SECURITY — paywall gate change (MV-dashboard-001)
per_check.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] apps/web/src/components/subscription-gate.tsx:57-92. KNOWN_DASHBOARD_SECTIONS (13 entries: agents, analytics, applications, approvals, cover-letters, email, interviews, jobs, networking, offers, resume, settings, stories) exactly matches `find apps/web/src/app/dashboard -name page.tsx` at 23ad8e6 (13 real page.tsx dirs, excluding [...slug] and __tests__) — no drift. isGateExempt() only returns true for a /dashboard/* path when it is NOT one of these prefixes (or a subpath of one), which structurally can only be true for a URL Next.js's own App Router would already resolve to the [...slug] catch-all (verified layout.tsx:31 wraps whatever `children` Next has already routed — SubscriptionGate does not itself do routing, so the exemption can never intercept real gated content matched by file-system routing). Ran the new vitest file apps/web/src/__tests__/dashboard/subscription-gate.test.tsx: 'still gates a real, known dashboard section for a free user' (/dashboard/jobs) passes — paywall still shown, no leak. Additionally reverted subscription-gate.tsx to its pre-fix version inside the fix branch's own worktree and re-ran the new 'unmapped route reachable' test: FAILED pre-fix (paywall shown instead of children) — confirms the test is a genuine regression guard, not tautological. File restored via cp; worktree verified clean (git status --short empty) afterward. No leak of paid features to free users found.

per_check.name: Honesty (tooltips/labels)
per_check.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Most copy fixes are genuinely accurate: DashboardStats.tsx:49-53 tooltip now says 'every status past draft (screening, interview, offer, or rejected)' matching funnel.applied's real semantics; MarketPulse.tsx:150-159 empty-state copy for Top Skills matches the app's existing honest-empty-state pattern; ATS-distribution/Agent-ROI '(all time — not affected by the period selector)' labels (analytics/page.tsx:226-229, 260-264) are accurate since those two backend endpoints genuinely take no period param. HOWEVER: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx:92, the edited 'Applications' tooltip reads '...across all sources and time periods.' This is now FALSE. This same diff (MV-analytics-004 fix) makes fetchDashboard(period) forward the selector to GET /analytics/dashboard?period=..., and the backend's _dashboard() (analytics.py:606-619) applies app_filter (period-derived) to total_apps. Live-verified with a scratch pytest probe against the fix branch (2 apps seeded now + 2 apps seeded 40 days ago, all submitted): GET /analytics/dashboard?period=all -> totalApplications=2; ?period=7d -> totalApplications=1 (probe file removed after run, worktree left clean, git status --short empty). So selecting '7d'/'30d'/'90d' now genuinely changes the 'Applications' card, directly contradicting its own tooltip's 'and time periods' claim. Compounding this, the dashboard-summary section (page.tsx:140) carries NO period indicator in its heading at all, unlike the sibling 'Application funnel ({period})' (line 162) and 'Stage conversion ({period})' (line 191) sections which do. Notably 'Agent Runs'/'Agent Spend' tooltips (page.tsx:97-98) already correctly say 'in this period' — showing the developer knew the right pattern but did not apply it to the one tooltip (Applications) this diff itself edited. This is a genuine, in-scope regression: MV-analytics-004/005 are not actually fully honest post-fix.

per_check.name: Tests fail-before/pass-after (not tautological)
per_check.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] apps/api/tests/test_analytics.py::test_applications_total_consistent_across_dashboard_funnel_market_pulse: copied verbatim onto the pre-fix source (c5ae389) in a disposable worktree and ran it — FAILED with `assert 7 == 4` (market_pulse's you_apps_month wrongly included the 3 drafts), confirming genuine fail-before. Ran full post-fix suite: passes (29/29 across test_analytics/test_applications_tracker/test_workspaces). apps/web/src/__tests__/dashboard/subscription-gate.test.tsx new 'unmapped route reachable' test: reverted subscription-gate.tsx to pre-fix inside the fix-branch worktree, re-ran — FAILED (paywall rendered, ACTIONABLE text absent); restored file, re-ran — passes. apps/web/src/components/analytics/__tests__/MarketPulse.test.tsx and .../tracker-lib.test.ts and dashboard/analytics/__tests__/page.test.tsx: inspected assertions directly (exact fixture values e.g. 'assert you_apps_month != total', pendingApprovalIds Set-driven filter assertions, apiRequest called with exact `?period=7d` querystring) — none are tautological placeholders; each asserts a concrete value that the pre-fix code path would produce differently. All 4 vitest files run green post-fix (29 tests).

per_check.name: Scope/prohibited patterns
per_check.note: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] Diff is minimal and scoped (14 files, +478/-49, matches the 7 named findings). `git show --stat 23ad8e6 | grep -i node_modules` → empty (clean); `git ls-tree -r 23ad8e6 --name-only | grep -i node_modules` → empty. No @ts-ignore/broad `as any`/Math.random/TODO/FIXME introduced (grepped the full diff). One `eslint-disable-next-line import/first` in the new analytics/__tests__/page.test.tsx — pre-existing, established codebase convention (17 other test files already use the identical suppression for the same vi.mock-before-import hoisting reason); not a new/dangerous pattern. No evidence of --no-verify in the single commit. HOWEVER: apps/api/app/routers/applications.py:39-41 funnel_sankey() was left untouched, and on inspection it is NOT a sound 'distinct semantic' call — `applied = status_counts.get('submitted', 0)` counts only rows whose literal status equals 'submitted', which UNDERCOUNTS relative to the canonical 'submitted' definition (status <> 'draft') this very diff establishes as ground truth everywhere else (e.g. an application that has progressed to 'screening'/'interview'/'offer' is silently dropped from the Sankey's 'Applied' stage count). This is reachable on the SAME page (dashboard/applications, 'Sankey Flow' view — see applications/page.tsx:600-621) that MV-application-tracker-002 targeted, and is the same class of divergent-inline-count bug the new helper's own docstring (analytics.py:31-43) claims to have eliminated ('single source of truth every applications figure... must derive from', 'Every surface below now calls this one function instead') — an overclaim, since funnel_sankey is not updated and is not 'below' in the same file. No finding ID in MANUAL-VERIFICATION-GAPS.json currently names this (grepped all findings for 'sankey' — none), so it is a newly-observed, previously-unfiled gap rather than a target of this fix; flagged for follow-up rather than treated as scope creep by the author. Recommend filing a new MV finding rather than expanding this diff.

blocking_issues: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx:92 — 'Applications' summary-card tooltip still claims '...across all sources and time periods', which is now FALSE: this same diff wires fetchDashboard(period) to forward the period selector (page.tsx:58) and analytics.py:606-619 _dashboard() genuinely scopes total_apps by app_filter (period-derived). Live-probed on the fix branch: totalApplications=2 for period=all vs totalApplications=1 for period=7d with identical seed data. Fix: either scope the tooltip text to the selected period (matching the already-correct pattern in 'Agent Runs'/'Agent Spend': '...in this period') or add a period indicator to the dashboard-summary section heading (it currently has none, unlike the sibling 'Application funnel ({period})' and 'Stage conversion ({period})' sections at lines 162/191). This must be resolved before MV-analytics-004 and MV-analytics-005 can be marked VERIFIED-CLOSED — the fix trades one dishonest-copy defect for another, still-open one.

apps/api/app/routers/applications.py:39-41 funnel_sankey() computes 'applied' via status_counts.get('submitted', 0) — a literal-status match that undercounts relative to the canonical 'submitted' (status<>'draft') definition this diff establishes everywhere else, on the same Applications-tracker page (Sankey Flow view). Not one of the 7 named findings and not introduced by this diff, but it is the same bug class the diff's own docstring claims to have fully eliminated. Recommend filing a new MV finding (e.g. MV-application-tracker-005) rather than silently leaving the 'single source of truth' claim in analytics.py:31-43 overstated. Does not block the 7 named findings but should not be treated as closed for the broader data-consistency epic.
positive_findings_for_the_record: get_application_counts() helper (analytics.py:31-68) is correctly designed, safe from injection, and genuinely consumed by all 3 named functions with no leftover divergent inline queries.

subscription-gate.tsx fix for MV-dashboard-001 is structurally sound and does not leak paid content to free users — verified both by static routing analysis and by fail-before/pass-after test execution in this run.

MV-analytics-006 / MV-mobile-dashboard-006 Top Skills empty-state fix, MV-application-tracker-002 needs-approval/pending-approvals set-consistency fix, and the DashboardStats tooltip honesty fix are all correct, minimal, and backed by genuine non-tautological tests.

29/29 backend pytest and 29/29 frontend vitest targeted tests pass on the fix branch; no prohibited patterns (no new @ts-ignore/eslint-disable-for-safety/any-casts/Math.random/TODO), no tracked node_modules, no --no-verify evidence.
